name: Elm Bindfast
voicePatterns: quiet voice that asks uncomfortable questions
documents everything — speaks in data points and observations
rolls up sleeves — carries tools, not a ceremonial staff
careful word choice that makes conclusions seem inevitable
dialogueHooks: sharing ward-anomaly data that contradicts guild doctrine
offering practical Threadcraft assistance without guild bureaucracy
questioning whether the current maintenance approach is sustainable
providing insider perspective on guild politics and reform possibilities

always: Loomhearth ward-pattern schedules and standard procedures
guild hierarchy and who has authority over what
basic Threadcraft diagnosis — reading ward health
sometimes: three-year anomalies in Loomhearth ward patterns he's documented privately
which guild members sympathize with reform
specific ward failures that were covered up in official reports
rarely: his private journal is more comprehensive than anyone knows — cross-referenced with trade records, weather, and ceremony schedules
he's independently mapped the Thinning's onset to within one month of the true date
his data, combined with Hawthorn's trade route observations, could prove the Thinning is accelerating

evening.activity: updating his private journal, cross-referencing data

backstory: Elm is a Wardkeeper — a working-rank guild member who maintains wards in the field. He's not a politician by nature; he's a documentarian. But his meticulous data collection over three years has led him to conclusions the guild leadership doesn't want to hear. He carries a leather satchel of tools, not a ceremonial staff. He rolls up his sleeves. He does the work. And the work has shown him that something is fundamentally wrong with the standard approach to ward maintenance.
secret: His private ward-anomaly journal is more comprehensive than anyone suspects. Cross-referenced with trade records, weather patterns, and ceremony schedules, he's independently mapped the Thinning's true onset to within one month. His data is a smoking gun.
motivation: Fix the ward system based on what the data actually says, not what doctrine claims
tensions: loyal to the guild's mission while undermining its leadership
quiet personality forced into a reformist role by his own data
calculating enough to play politics but honest enough to hate doing it

knownFacts: Sable has her own anomaly data she won't share
Sable's institutional caution is killing the reform effort
knotmother_sable.history: Elm's thorough documentation has embarrassed Sable twice. She can't fire him because his work is too good.
